steps.expected: All listed locks present in DAG
steps.observed: All 8 required lock categories covered plus 5 additional (page_allocator, memory, serial, address_space_id, slab)

evidence.sourceVerification: grep confirmed 13 IrqMutex instances matching documentation

Fix 17 documented in safety-map.md §2: TPIDR written under scheduler lock in schedule_inner, exception.S writes are redundant defense-in-depth. 3000-key stress test validation noted.
4 read sites: save_context (exception.S:223), exc_fatal (exception.S:193), handler_returned_null (exception.S:402), kernel_fault_handler (main.rs:541)

evidence.sourceVerification: grep confirmed 6 write sites in scheduler.rs and exception.S, zero in boot.S

steps.expected: All 6 handle types with creation/transfer/close/cleanup traced
steps.observed: 6 handle types documented in 6 sections: Channel (§1), Timer (§2), Interrupt (§3), Thread (§4), Process (§5), SchedulingContext (§6). Each has Creation, Transfer, Close, Process Exit Cleanup, and Leak Analysis subsections.

steps.action: Verify files traced: handle.rs, syscall.rs, process.rs, channel.rs
steps.observed: Document references handle.rs, syscall.rs, process.rs, channel.rs, timer.rs, interrupt.rs, thread_exit.rs, process_exit.rs, scheduler.rs

steps.action: Verify leak-free conclusion for each type
steps.observed: All 6 types have 'Leak Analysis: No leaks. ✅'. Cross-cutting verification section covers handle drain completeness, rollback completeness, handle_send rollback, and double-close prevention.

steps.expected: Tests related to handle lifecycle exist
steps.observed: Document references system/test/tests/handle_lifecycle.rs with 30 tests

steps.expected: Both normal exit and kill paths with all resource types
steps.observed: Three paths documented: Path 1 (normal exit, 5 phases), Path 2 (kill, 4 phases), Path 3 (deferred cleanup for killed processes with running threads on other cores)

steps.expected: Handles, channels, timers, interrupts, scheduling contexts, DMA, heap, page tables, TLB, ASID
steps.observed: 15 resource categories documented: handles (6 types), scheduling context refs, internal timeout timers, futex wait entries, DMA buffers, owned page frames, heap metadata, page table frames (L1-L3), L0 page table, TLB entries, ASID, kernel stack, thread object, process slot, waiter registrations (soft leak)

Detailed phase-by-phase ordering for all 3 paths. Normal exit: Phase 1 (scheduler lock, release sched ctx refs, collect timeout timer, drain handles) → Phase 2 (notify exits, remove futex) → Phase 3 (close handle resources) → Phase 4 (TLB invalidate, free address space, release ASID) → Phase 5 (mark exited, reschedule, deferred drop).
Bug found and fixed: internal timeout timer leak. 18 model-based tests added. Soft leak (stale waiter registrations) documented as acceptable with justification.

steps.expected: Sections for: lock ordering, TPIDR_EL1, handle lifecycle, process cleanup, allocator routing, ASID lifecycle
steps.observed: 9 sections present: §1 Lock Ordering DAG, §2 TPIDR_EL1 Invariant Chain, §3 Handle Lifecycle Per Type, §4 Process Exit Cleanup, §5 Allocator Routing, §6 ASID Lifecycle, §7 Scheduling Context ref_count, §8 Emergency Stack Sizing, §9 Cross-Cutting Invariant Summary

steps.expected: Lock ordering, TPIDR_EL1, handle lifecycle, process cleanup, allocator routing, ASID lifecycle
steps.observed: All 6 required topics present as dedicated sections (§1-§6). Plus 3 additional sections covering scheduling context ref_count, emergency stack sizing, and a 22-invariant cross-cutting summary.

§9 summarizes 22 cross-cutting invariants with verification method and violation symptom for each. Document covers 35 .rs + 2 .S source files. References companion documents (LOCK-ORDERING.md, TPIDR-CHAIN.md, DESIGN.md).

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. Lock ordering DAG covers 13 locks with cycle-freedom proof. TPIDR_EL1 invariant chain traces 6 write sites (confirmed against kernel source grep). Handle lifecycle documents all 6 types as leak-free. Process exit cleanup covers 15 resource categories across 3 exit paths. Safety invariant map has 9 sections covering all required topics plus 22 cross-cutting invariants.
